commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=8ea8e88574e8e3cbf76d3f5deb6bb78ce62e2165)
commands.review_summary:
The PR decouples Codex app-server user MCP server attachment from native code mode, filters projected user MCP servers by the effective tool allowlist, emits Codex `enabled_tools` for exact tool entries, and updates related docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +132, Tests +149, Docs +19. Total +300 across 8 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers at source level: PR-head projection reserves disabled server names before filtering, and the changed docs contradict the PR-head `enabled_tools` behavior. I did not run a live Codex app-server scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Manual follow-up is needed because the external contributor must add live runtime proof and maintainers should decide the security-boundary/product policy after the concrete fixes.

target.title: feat(codex): scope user MCP server attachment by the tool allowlist
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

An OpenClaw operator running a **Codex-runtime agent** cannot give that agent access to a configured MCP server without also granting it Codex's full native code-mode shell/filesystem surface. The two are conflated behind a single gate.

In `extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.ts`, `shouldEnableCodexAppServerNativeToolSurface` returns true only when the effective tool allowlist contains a literal `*`. That one boolean (`nativeToolSurfaceEnabled`) is then reused, unchanged, in `attempt-startup.ts` as `userMcpServersEnabled` — which decides whether `buildCodexUserMcpServersThreadConfigPatch` projects `cfg.mcp.servers` into the Codex thread at all. Consequences:

1. **All-or-nothing.** Any least-privilege allowlist (`tools.allow` / `tools.alsoAllow` without `*`) silently disables **every** user MCP server, even ones the allowlist explicitly names. The MCP process spawns but its tools never register in the compiled toolset.
2. **Over-exposure when servers do attach.** `buildCodexUserMcpServersThreadConfigPatch` projected **every** enabled `cfg.mcp.servers` entry, filtered only by the per-server `codex.agents` field — it never consulted the tool allowlist.

This is a general gap for any scoped Codex agent with any MCP server, not a single integration.

## What Changed

Decouple the two capabilities and make MCP attachment flow through the **same allowlist contract OpenClaw already uses for every other tool** (`tool-policy.ts`):

- **Native code mode is unchanged** — `shouldEnableCodexAppServerNativeToolSurface` keeps its fail-closed, wildcard-only rule. The dangerous, sandbox-sensitive surface is no less locked down.
- **`shouldEnableUserMcpServersForCodexAppServer`** now answers only *"is it safe to attach external MCP servers this turn?"* (not a memory-flush run; OpenClaw sandbox, if active, can be honored). The per-server decision moves to the projection.
- **`resolveCodexMcpServerAllow`** matches against the **provider-safe model-facing tool ids** OpenClaw actually exposes — `<sanitizedServer>__<sanitizedTool>` via `sanitizeServerName` + `normalizeToolName`, **not** the raw `mcp.servers` key — and honors the same entries as the existing bundle-MCP allowlist contract:
  - `undefined` allowlist → include, all tools (legacy unrestricted behavior preserved)
  - `*`, `bundle-mcp`, `group:plugins` → include, all tools
  - `<server>__*` → include that server, all tools
  - `<server>__<tool>` → include that server, scoped to the named tool(s)
  - no matching token → exclude the server
- **`buildCodexUserMcpServersThreadConfigPatch`** projects **only** the servers the allowlist references, and for exact-tool entries emits Codex's per-server **`enabled_tools`** filter so other tools from an attached server stay unreachable. The thread-config fingerprint varies with the patch contents, so a thread rebinds when the allowlist changes which servers/tools are in scope.

### Addresses ClawSweeper review

- **Provider-safe matching** (was raw-key): a server keyed `Outlook Graph` (tool ids `outlook-graph__*`) now resolves correctly, and `bundle-mcp` / `group:plugins` allowlist entries are honored.
- **Per-tool scope** (was discarded): exact `<server>__<tool>` entries now emit `enabled_tools` instead of attaching the whole server. (`enabled_tools` carries the sanitized tool fragment; exact for provider-safe tool names and fail-closed otherwise, since the live tool list isn't known at projection time.)

## Files

- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.ts` — decoupled `shouldEnableUserMcpServersForCodexAppServer`
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts`, `attempt-startup.ts`, `thread-lifecycle.ts` — plumb `userMcpServersEnabled` separately, pass `toolsAllow` into the projection
- `src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.ts` — `resolveCodexMcpServerAllow` + allowlist-aware projection with `enabled_tools`
- `docs/plugins/codex-harness.md` — documents allowlist-driven MCP attachment
- tests as below

## Evidence

Unit tests (run in the worktree against the repo toolchain, `vitest run`):

- `src/agents/cli-runner/bundle-mcp-codex.user-config.test.ts` — **24 passed**. Covers: undefined/`*`/`bundle-mcp`/`group:plugins` attach all; `opik__*` attaches only opik with no `enabled_tools`; exact `opik__list,opik__read` emits `enabled_tools: ["list","read"]`; provider-safe matching (`Outlook Graph` ↔ `outlook-graph__*`); `["message"]`/`[]` attach nothing; `resolveCodexMcpServerAllow` truth table incl. bare-prefix rejection.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/dynamic-tool-build.test.ts` — **51 passed**. Native surface stays wildcard-only while the MCP gate is independent; memory-flush attaches no MCP servers.
- `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle*.test.ts` + `attempt-startup.test.ts` — green with the new plumbing.

Type safety: standalone `tsc -p extensions/codex/tsconfig.json` shows only pre-existing plugin-sdk `dist`-sync errors (CI rebuilds the SDK first); none reference symbols this PR adds/changes. Full-CI `check-prod-types` / `check-lint` / `check-docs` pass.

> ⏳ **Runtime proof still pending** (the `status: needs proof` ask): a redacted Codex app-server capture showing a scoped allowlist (`opik__*`) attaching only the intended tools with native code mode disabled. Will be added from a live run before merge.

## Security posture

- Native code-mode shell/file access still requires a literal `*` — unchanged.
- Default-open behavior for unrestricted agents (`undefined` allowlist) preserved.
- Sandbox honoring preserved: an active OpenClaw sandbox without the exec-server path attaches no user MCP servers.
- Net effect is *tighter*: scoped allowlists attach only the referenced servers, and exact-tool entries attach only the named tools via `enabled_tools`.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)
target.author: jleonelion
